_meta.artifact: ADR-013 — owner trade-usage review of the common-name -> binomial worklist mappings
_meta.source: owner + trade-review pass over the private-catalogue common-name mappings
_meta.note: Corrections are resolved mappings to apply. trade_ambiguous entries must NOT resolve to a single binomial in the common-name mapping round — they carry into the backbone with candidate_accepted_names and require source/provenance, mirroring the existing Pass-2 trade_ambiguous handling (e.g. Cinnamomum cassia). No silent first-match.

corrections.note: Giyol = Giloy / Guduchi (Tinospora cordifolia); Sesbania was a misidentification. Guduchi was otherwise absent from the whole catalogue. Sesbania is already covered by 'Sesbania S' -> Sesbania grandiflora.
Sichuan pepper in trade is Zanthoxylum bungeanum; Z. piperitum is Japanese sansho, a different commodity.

trade_ambiguous.why_flagged: The diosgenin 'Mexican wild yam' (barbasco) is D. composita / D. mexicana; D. villosa is the North-American wild yam. Species depends on which material.
Classic multi-species Ayurvedic name; region-dependent (N. India Convolvulus, Bengal Clitoria, S. India Evolvulus, also Canscora).
'Gond' is a generic edible gum; source tree varies (gum arabic = Senegalia senegal, babool gond = Vachellia nilotica, ghatti = Anogeissus latifolia, katira = Sterculia urens).
Kashmiri ratanjot (rogan-josh colour) is usually Arnebia/Onosma; European alkanet = Alkanna tinctoria. (Jatropha curcas is a separate unrelated 'ratanjot'.)
Notoriously adulterated Brazilian trade name; multiple unrelated species sold as 'catuaba'.
Chinese skullcap (baicalensis, Huang Qin) vs American skullcap (lateriflora, Western herbalism). Bare 'Skullcap' is ambiguous.
'Kelp' is properly Laminaria / Ascophyllum; Fucus vesiculosus is bladderwrack (rockweed). The name and species disagree in trade.
Cedarwood-oil source varies: Himalayan deodar (C. deodara), Atlas (C. atlantica), Virginia (Juniperus virginiana).
Indian gooseberry (amla = Phyllanthus emblica) vs European gooseberry (Ribes uva-crispa). Bare 'Gooseberry' is ambiguous; amla is already mapped from 'Amla'.
American wormseed / epazote (Dysphania ambrosioides) vs Levant wormseed / santonin (Artemisia cina).
Mesquite flour in trade is usually Prosopis pallida / glandulosa; P. juliflora is the invasive species.
Rosewood essential oil = Aniba rosaeodora; 'rosewood' as timber/other extracts = Dalbergia. Fine if oil-only, but the bare name is ambiguous.
